 Inom vår IT-organisation är du med och utvecklar Sverige med hjälp av moderna och innovativa lösningar. Vi är mitt i en spännande förändring där vi försöker ta vara på digitaliseringens möjligheter till fullo. Digitaliseringen ställer allt större krav och vi vill därför anpassa oss vad gäller kompetens, arbetssätt och organisation i takt med utvecklingen. Vi har ett helhetsansvar för IT och telekom, en verksamhet som pågår dygnet runt, året om - precis som Sveriges transportsystem.
   

  

 På Trafikverket jobbar vi med att göra Sverige närmare. Vi tror på att en förening av olika erfarenheter, bakgrunder och perspektiv är den bästa grunden för att bygga en arbetsplats fri från diskriminering och en miljö där alla trivs och gör sitt bästa. Vi tror på mångfald där den gemensamma nämnaren är pålitlighet, engagemang och mod. För det är så vi bäst representerar vårt samhälle.

title: Software Monitoring Specialist (Automotive) – 17577
description:
**Note : Applicants must be based in Sweden with a valid work permit and Swedish fluency.** 




  





**Assignment Description** 




 We are looking for a Software Monitoring Specialist
 



  





**What You Will Work On** 



* Own and continuously improve monitoring, dashboards, and alerting for SWS
* Enhance observability practices based on real user and workshop needs
* Use monitoring tools to perform initial incident investigation and troubleshooting
* Support and participate in major incident management processes
* Route and coordinate issues with relevant development teams
* Contribute to root cause analysis and follow-up improvement initiatives
* Collaborate with development teams to improve system reliability and visibility
* Communicate effectively with stakeholders across technical and business teams



  





**What You Bring** 



* Experience working with monitoring and observability in software systems
* Hands-on experience with tools such as Datadog or similar platforms
* Strong interest in IT systems, operations, and troubleshooting
* Ability to use dashboards and monitoring tools for incident investigation
* Fluent in English and Swedish (spoken and written)
* Availability for on-call support (24/7 rotation schedule)
* Strong collaboration and communication skills

title: System Engineer
description:
**Company Description**

 Multiply Teknik & IT is a dynamic and innovative company specializing in Engineering and IT solutions, delivering expertise across industrial, healthcare, and engineering sectors. We focus on creating and enhancing customer value through our cutting-edge offerings, which include VisFlow, a leading flow visualization and analytics platform, as well as services in business process consulting, functional safety, cybersecurity, and cloud solutions. Our portfolio also encompasses advanced engineering services like model-based design and mechatronics, along with comprehensive IT services such as data analysis development. Based in Stockholm, we are dedicated to driving innovation and excellence for our clients.
   




**Role Description**

 This is a full-time, on-site System Engineer role based in Stockholm. As a System Engineer, you will be responsible for designing, implementing, and maintaining complex systems to support client requirements. Duties include system integration, troubleshooting, performance optimization, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver robust and efficient solutions. Additionally, you will contribute to system architecture design, ensure adherence to cybersecurity standards, and manage technical documentation.
   




**Qualifications**
* Proficiency in system architecture design, system integration, and troubleshooting
* Experience with functional safety, cybersecurity practices, and data governance
* Strong skills in cloud platforms such as AWS, Google Cloud, or Azure
* Expertise in engineering services including model-based definition and mechatronic design
* Demonstrated experience in IT services, particularly data analysis and development
* Excellent problem-solving abilities and collaborative skills for cross-disciplinary teamwork
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, or a related technical field
* Familiarity with industrial and healthcare industry standards is a plus

title: Infrastructure Engineer
description:
Om tjänsten
   

  

 I tjänsten som Infrastructure Engineer kommer du arbeta tillsammans med ett team i en modern IT-miljö. Du kommer att arbeta med att administrera och automatisera storskaliga system och vi söker efter dig som har arbetat med cloud, virtualisering och moderna lagringstekniker. Som Infrastructure Engineer är det en självklarhet att definiera infrastruktur som kod för att underlätta din och andras vardag. Vi ser att du, precis som vi, vill lära dig om, och jobba mer med containers och cloud samtidigt som det behöver fungera med en existerande on-prem lösning. Vi arbetar i GCP som molnplattform.
   

  

**Du Kommer Även Att Arbeta Med**
* Definiera och hantera infrastruktur som kod
* Hantera virtualiseringslösningar som Openshift Virtualization, HyperV
* Arbeta med moderna lagringstekniker


 Du kommer att ingå i vårt Infrastructure-team på 8 personer som tillsammans ser till att vi alltid levererar stabila och säkra tjänster till våra kunder. Du kommer få möjlighet att utvecklas i ett engagerat team som ger dig mycket förtroende och som team ansvarar man för att tillhandahålla våra bas-infrastrukturtjänster och även att ständigt framtidssäkra vår IT-miljö.
   

  

 Vem vi söker
   

  

 För att vara framgångsrik i rollen som Infrastructure Engineer hos oss bör du vara en nyfiken problemlösare med en passion för teknik och drivkraft att ständigt förbättra. Ditt engagemang att vilja lära dig mer om och hålla dig uppdaterad kring de senaste teknikerna är viktigt för att du ska ta dig an nya utmaningar. Vidare söker vi dig med en analytisk förmåga som kommer att vara avgörande i rollen för att effektivt optimera våra system och identifiera komplexa tekniska utmaningar som kan uppstå. Vi söker flera Infrastructure engineers med olika kompetensområden om du känner att du inte täcker in allt i beskrivningen.
   

  

**Vi Ser Gärna Att Du Har**
* Goda kunskaper inom datacenterinfrastruktur och plattformar som Openshift, Kubernetes eller HyperV
* God erfarenhet av Linuxsystemadministration (RHEL) i större skala
* Goda kunskaper inom någon av dom större public cloud (GCP, Azure, AWS)
* Kunskap i IaC-verktyg som Puppet, Ansible, Terraform


 Du ska trivas i en dynamisk miljö där förändring och innovation är en del av vardagen. Du har förmågan att snabbt anpassa dig till nya situationer och möjligheter som uppstår. Vi erbjuder dig en arbetsplats med mycket frihet att få pröva dina kunskaper på egen hand, men som även ser samarbetet med teamet som en värdefull del av ditt arbete. Vi tror på kraften i samarbete och kunskapsutbyte med kollegor för att tillsammans skapa framgångsrika projekt. Vi hoppas att du delar denna syn och vill bli en del av vårt Infrastructure-team.
   

  

 Ansökan
   

  

 Skicka din ansökan genom att klicka på knappen ”Ansök nu” under annonsen. Skicka gärna din ansökan snarast då urvalet sker löpande. Vi tar inte emot ansökningar via mail p.g.a. GDPR. Kontakta gärna Henrik Hjelm, Engineering Manager & Product Manager, på henrik.hjelm@avanza.se om du har några frågor om tjänsten.
   

  

 Välkommen med din ansökan!
   

  

 Erbjudanden från rekryteringsföretag/annonssäljare undanbedes.
   

  

 Avanza är Sveriges ledande plattform för sparande och placeringar. Vi drivs av att förändra branschen genom att utmana de stora bankerna och försäkringsbolagen på den svenska sparmarknaden. Vi vill vara tjänsten som förenklar och förbättrar spararnas ekonomi, oavsett om man älskar aktier eller bara vill ha ett bra sparande. Vår vision är att skapa en bättre framtid för miljoner människor. Därför står våra kunders bästa alltid i fokus, precis som det borde vara. Affärsidén är enkel – mer till dig, mindre till banken. En idé som gett oss Sveriges nöjdaste sparare 15 år i rad.

title: Sales Manager - Stockholm, Sweden
description:
**About FCM** 




 FCM is more than a global travel management company with 24/7 support in 100+ countries. We’re a movement—6,500+ changemakers, travel experts, and performance drivers redefining corporate travel. We don’t just keep the world’s biggest companies moving—we make it smoother, smarter, and bolder. Certified as a great place to work and fuelled by an alternative mindset, we embrace change, challenge convention, and do things differently. This is FCM—where different happens.
 



**About The Role** 




 As a Sales Manager, you’ll play a pivotal role in accelerating FCM’s growth in one of our fastest‑growing markets. You’ll own the full sales lifecycle, from identifying and engaging new prospects through to closing profitable, long‑term partnerships with Finnish small to mid‑sized organisations.
 



 Working closely with a Nordic and EMEA sales team, you’ll engage senior decision‑makers, deliver compelling value‑led proposals, and demonstrate how FCM’s market‑leading technology and service model help businesses perform better. While your primary focus will be Finland, this role offers future opportunities to support growth across the wider Nordic region.
 



 You’ll also be responsible for building a strong pipeline, increasing FCM’s brand presence in the Finnish market, and contributing valuable local market insight to regional sales strategy.
 



**About You** 




 You’re an experienced B2B sales professional with a strong track record of winning complex, multi‑stakeholder deals in the Finnish market. You understand local business culture, procurement processes, and how to influence senior stakeholders with confidence and credibility.
 



 You bring:
 


* 4+ years’ experience in B2B sales, including success in closing mid‑market or enterprise‑level contracts
* Proven ability to manage the full sales cycle, from prospecting to negotiation and close
* Strong presentation, communication, and relationship‑building skills
* An existing Finnish business network and the ability to secure senior‑level meetings
* Fluency in Swedish and English
* Experience using CRM tools such as Salesforce and a structured, data‑driven approach to pipeline management
* A proactive, self‑driven mindset with the ambition to grow both personally and commercially



 If you’re motivated by growth, autonomy, and the opportunity to shape FCM’s presence in Finland, this is a chance to make a real impact.
 



 Our number one philosophy is Our people. Flight Centre Travel Group's promise is to provide an environment with equality of respect, dignity and opportunity for all our employees. We value an inclusive and supportive workplace which reflects the diversity of our society.
 



 We welcome accommodation requests to help make our hiring and onboarding experience as accessible as possible. Please advise us about accommodation needs at any point by contacting our Recruitment Team at RecruitmentUK@flightcentre.co.uk .
 



 #FCM

title: Global Quality Coordinator at Vitamin Well Group
description:
*At* 
***Vitamin Well Group,***
*we provide great-tasting drinks, snacks, and protein products. Our brands Vitamin Well, NOCCO, and Barebells are available across most of Europe, North America, the MENA region, and parts of Asia Pacific. With a revenue of 650 million euros, we operate in over 40 markets, with headquarters in Stockholm and offices in fourteen countries around the world.*
 We are now looking for a
 **Global Quality Coordinator** 
 to join our Global Quality team at HQ in Stockholm. As we continue to grow across markets, suppliers, and production setups, maintaining structure and alignment across quality activities becomes increasingly important. This role plays an important part in keeping quality activities, documentation, and product follow-up structured and moving forward across markets and suppliers.
   

  

**About the role**
 You will join a global environment where quality, pace, and collaboration all matter, and where you will work closely with experienced colleagues across Quality, Product Development, Regulatory and Sourcing. You will report to the Global Head of Product Development & Quality and be based at our Stockholm headquarters.
   

  

 Working closely with the Global Quality Specialists, you will coordinate and support quality activities connected to incidents, testing, supplier follow-up, product launches and production across multiple markets, production partners and vendors.
   

  

 This role is suited for someone early in their career who wants to grow within quality in an international food and beverage environment.
   

  

 In short you will:
   

  

* Support quality-related activities across suppliers, production sites, and internal stakeholders
* Manage product sample flows, testing activities, and related follow-up connected to product development and production
* Maintain and update quality documentation, specifications, internal systems, and ongoing quality records
* Coordinate activities connected to deviations, claims, and other quality cases
* Support product launches from a quality and documentation perspective across multiple markets and production partners
* Work closely with Product Development, Regulatory, Sourcing, and Operations in the day-to-day coordination of quality activities


 What you can expect
   

  

* A hands-on role where you will develop your skills within quality operations in the food and beverage industry
* Exposure to a fast-moving global environment with frequent launches across drinks, functional snacks, and product extensions
* Daily collaboration with experienced quality professionals and close cross-functional work across Product Development, Regulatory and Sourcing
* A company where pace, collaboration, and quality matter — and where you are trusted to contribute and grow over time


**Who are you**
 You are likely early in your career within quality in the food and beverage industry, and looking for an opportunity to develop in an international environment with real products, suppliers, and product launches.
   

  

 You are structured, reliable, and comfortable handling details without losing track of the bigger picture. You enjoy coordination, follow-up, and creating order in environments where many things happen simultaneously.
   

  

 You are collaborative, curious, and motivated by learning how products move from development to production and market launch.
   

  

 Essential skills to succeed:
   

  

* Around 1–2 years of experience from quality-related roles connected to food and/or beverage products — including areas such as food safety, product quality, testing, specifications, supplier quality, or quality documentation. Internships and other practical experience are also relevant
* University degree within food science, nutrition, chemistry, biotechnology, or related field
* Strong attention to detail and ability to work in a structured way across multiple ongoing activities
* Ability to coordinate and follow up on several ongoing activities with accuracy and structure
* Comfortable working cross-functionally in a fast-moving international environment
* Practical and hands-on approach, with the ability to balance coordination, follow-up, and administration
* Professional level of both Swedish and English, written and spoken


**Welcome with your application at the latest June 7th 2026.**
*We will review applications on an ongoing basis and the position may be filled before the final application day. We appreciate LinkedIn, but to get a full picture of your experience, we ask you to also include a CV.*

**Life at Vitamin Well Group**
 Our growing family is made up of over 600 employees, representing a variety of cultures, experiences and diverse backgrounds. This diversity is a great asset as we continue our expansion and evolve our portfolio. At the same time, Vitamin Well is still very much a Swedish company, promoting Swedish values when it comes to both business and people. We treat each other in a fair and equal way, and we respect one another based on our qualities. As we expand Vitamin Well Group’s business, we are also committed to minimising our environmental impact and continuously strive to lower emissions from for example production, logistics, and packaging.
   

  

 Working at Vitamin Well, we provide a dynamic environment defined by ambition, dedication, and an entrepreneurial spirit. In our action-oriented culture, the ability to make swift decisions is paramount in bringing ideas to fruition. We are committed to fostering an atmosphere where every individual's unique strengths contribute to our collective success. As a health and exercise-oriented company, we inspire an active lifestyle by partnering with top-performing athletes, but we also promote and support exercise among our employees.
   

  

*As part of the recruitment process, we may ask you to conduct a personality test as well as a test that measures logical ability. The purpose of the tests is to evaluate candidates as objective as possible and identify the best possible match for the role. The tests are based on solid research and are taken through our partner Alva Labs.*

title: Kravanalytiker till försvarskund
description:
**Vill du vara med och bygga framtidens lösningar inom offentlig sektor och försvar?**
 Har du flera års erfarenhet av att arbeta nära utvecklingsteam och en förmåga att omvandla komplexa tekniska behov till tydliga krav? Då kan du vara den kravanalytiker vi söker.
   

  

 För Nexer är varje utmaning en möjlighet och som konsult hos oss kommer du få utveckling genom dessa möjligheter, både inom olika tekniker och olika branscher. Du får en stabil och trygg anställning samtidigt som du får möjlighet att testa och utveckla din kompetens i olika uppdrag. Vi tror på en långsiktig och hållbar anställning där vi lägger stor vikt vid både kompetensutveckling och work life balance.
   

  

**Vem är du?**
 Vi letar efter dig som har minst 9 års erfarenhet som kravanalytiker och som har utvecklat en djup kompetens inom området. Du är van att arbeta självständigt, ta ansvar för delområden och agera förebild för andra konsulter. Du har en naturlig förmåga att leda mindre grupper och att skapa struktur i komplexa projektmiljöer.
   

  

 I rollen kommer du att:
   

  

* Samla in, analysera och dokumentera krav från olika intressenter
* Arbeta nära systemutvecklare med fokus på integrationer, API:er och dataflöden
* Ta fram tekniska och icke-funktionella krav kopplade till prestanda, säkerhet, tillgänglighet och driftsbarhet
* Samverka med lösningsarkitekter och IT-drift för att säkerställa att kraven är genomförbara och välförankrade
* Tolka tekniska dokument som arkitekturbeskrivningar, sekvensdiagram och datamodeller, och omsätta dessa till funktionella krav


 Du har god vana av att arbeta med etablerade verktyg och metoder, och är trygg i att uttrycka dig på svenska i både tal och skrift.
   

  

 För rollen krävs svenskt medborgarskap samt mycket god förmåga att kommunicera på svenska, både muntligt och skriftligt. Uppdraget är förlagt hos kund i Enköping och innebär fysisk närvaro på plats fem dagar i veckan.
   

  

**Om Nexer Public Sales**
 Affärsområdet Public Sales på Nexer har länge arbetat med att driva och utveckla våra uppdrag inom offentlig sektor – myndigheter, regioner och kommuner. Nu tar vi nästa steg och skapar en ännu starkare plattform och närvaro i Mälardalsregionen. Hos oss får du inte bara arbeta med samhällsviktiga uppdrag – du får också vara med och påverka, forma kulturen och bygga upp något tillsammans med oss.
   

  

**Ansökan**
 Du är varmt välkommen att söka tjänsten genom att skicka in din ansökan här nedan. Enligt GDPR hanterar vi inte ansökningar via e-post men har du frågor om tjänsten eller Nexer är du välkommen att höra av dig till ansvarig rekryterare, Xie Gamboa, xie.gamboa@nexergroup.com.
   

  

 Vi går igenom ansökningar löpande, så vänta inte med din ansökan!
   

  

 Läs mer om Nexer här
   

  

 www.nexergroup.com

title: Managing Partner - Manufacturing, Industrial Products, Retail, Supply Chain
description:
**Managing Partner – Commercial Sectors (Sweden)**
**Manufacturing | Industrial Products | Retail | Supply Chain**
**IT Strategy & Digital Transformation**
**Stockholm (Hybrid)**
 Backed by the unparalleled insight of Gartner Research, Gartner Consulting enables senior business and technology leaders to navigate disruption with confidence. We combine objective, data‑driven insight with deep industry expertise to help organisations accelerate digital transformation and realise measurable value.
   

  

 As part of our continued investment in the Nordic region, we are seeking an exceptional Managing Partner to accelerate the growth of our Commercial Sectors business in Sweden. This role will complement and collaborate with our existing Partner team, expanding our capabilities, strengthening our industry coverage, and deepening our portfolio of high‑impact client engagements.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As Managing Partner, you will be the strategic lead for some of Sweden’s most influential organisations across Manufacturing, Industrial Products, Retail and Supply Chain. You will build trusted relationships with executive stakeholders, shaping their technology and digital agendas and advising on mission‑critical priorities across IT Strategy, digital transformation, applications modernisation, cloud strategy, and value realisation.
   

  

 You will have the commercial acumen to grow a substantial client portfolio, the capability to engage credibly at C‑suite level, and the consulting pedigree to oversee delivery of complex transformation programmes. This is a role for someone who can operate as a trusted consulting partner, combine strong commercial acumen with senior‑level advisory capability, and shape forward‑looking technology initiatives that translate into measurable business impact.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
 In this role, you will:
   

  

* Lead and expand Gartner’s consulting footprint within Sweden’s commercial sectors, driving growth beyond current capabilities and offerings.
* Build and sustain trusted, strategic partnerships with CEOs, CIOs, CTOs, CFOs, CDOs and senior transformation leaders.
* Shape and deliver engagements across IT Strategy (including operating models, architecture, governance and digital roadmaps), digital transformation and emerging technology adoption.
* Work closely with Gartner Research to anchor transformation programs in evidence‑based insight, while leading clients through the execution of digital and IT strategies, value‑realisation initiatives and complex transformation programmes.
* Oversee engagement delivery, ensuring measurable value, execution quality and alignment with client objectives.
* Lead sales pursuits, create compelling proposals and develop long‑term account strategies.
* Collaborate with other Nordic and EMEA Partners to strengthen cross‑sector offerings and expand our market presence.
* Mentor and support our growing Swedish Consulting team, contributing to a high‑performance, collaborative culture.


**What You Bring**
 You may currently be a Managing Director, Client Partner, Executive Director or equivalent senior leader in a top‑tier consulting firm. Alternatively, you may already be operating at Partner level and seeking the opportunity to shape a rapidly expanding Nordic practice.
   

  

 We are looking for:
   

  

* A strong commercial and sales orientation: proven success in account development, pipeline growth, and building long‑term executive relationships.
* Deep IT and management consulting experience, enabling credible advisory at senior levels.
* Expertise across one or more of these industries; Manufacturing, Industrial Products, Retail or Supply Chain sectors in Sweden/Nordics.
* Experience delivering or overseeing: C‑suite advisory on digital and technology strategy
* Digital transformation
* Applications and cloud modernisation
* IT operating model design
* Value realisation and programme assurance
* A balanced capability across strategy, execution, technology and business change.
* Strong analytical, communication and executive‑level influencing skills.


**Why This Role is Unique**
* Significant greenfield opportunity, with vast room to expand the commercial sector footprint.
* Direct access to Gartner’s global research, benchmark data and technology insights, forming a unique value proposition unmatched in the market.
* Close collaboration with experienced Nordic Partners, with the autonomy to shape new offerings and lead major client relationships.
* Cross‑Nordic and European growth potential, supported by a highly scalable operating model.


**Why Join Us?**
 This is a unique opportunity to join a world-class consulting team with a proven track record, you’ll work alongside experts who are committed to delivering meaningful change.
   

  

**Our Team Culture**
 We believe that great work starts with great people. You’ll be part of a team that is anchored in shared values, deep technology expertise, and a strong sense of purpose. We foster a culture of trust, collaboration, and continuous learning—where every voice is valued and every contribution matters.
   

  

 We also believe in flexibility. Our consultants enjoy the freedom to work from wherever they perform best—whether that’s from home, the office, or on-site with clients. What unites us is not where we work, but how we work: with integrity, curiosity, and a commitment to making a difference.
   

  

**Who are we?**
 At Gartner, Inc. (NYSE:IT), we guide the leaders who shape the world.
   

  

 Our mission relies on expert analysis and bold ideas to deliver actionable, objective business and technology insights, helping enterprise leaders and their teams succeed with their mission-critical priorities.
   

  

 Since our founding in 1979, we’ve grown to 20,000 associates globally who support over 13,000 client enterprises in ~90 countries and territories. We do important, interesting and substantive work that matters. That’s why we hire associates with the intellectual curiosity, energy and drive to want to make a difference. The bar is unapologetically high. So is the impact you can have here.
   

  

**What makes Gartner a great place to work?**
 Our vast, virtually untapped market potential offers limitless opportunities – opportunities that may not even exist right now – for you to grow professionally and flourish personally. How far you go is driven by your passion and performance.
   

  

 We hire remarkable people who collaborate and win as a team. Together, our singular, unifying goal is to deliver results for our clients.
   

  

 Our teams are inclusive and composed of individuals from different geographies, cultures, religions, ethnicities, races, genders, sexual orientations, abilities and generations.
   

  

 We invest in great leaders who bring out the best in you and the company, enabling us to multiply our impact and results. This is why, year after year, we are recognized worldwide as a great place to work.
   

  

**Gartner is the world authority on AI**
 At Gartner, you’ll join a company at the very center of the AI revolution. Gartner has proactive, objective guidance throughout clients’ AI journeys. We set the standard for how organizations leverage artificial intelligence to drive meaningful impact. You’ll have access to unmatched resources, expertise, and technology, and play a key role in helping Gartner and our clients innovate and grow as we leverage AI to transform business and technology landscapes.
   

  

 It’s an exciting time to be at Gartner, with limitless opportunities to make a real impact, grow your skills, and build a lasting, meaningful career in a field that’s reshaping the way we operate. If you’re passionate about AI and want to be part of a team that’s guiding the leaders who shape the world, Gartner is the place for you.
   

  

**What do we offer?**
 Gartner offers world-class benefits, highly competitive compensation and disproportionate rewards for top performers.
   

  

 In our hybrid work environment, we provide the flexibility and support for you to thrive — working virtually when it's productive to do so and getting together with colleagues in a vibrant community that is purposeful, engaging and inspiring.
   

  

 Ready to grow your career with Gartner? Join us.
   

  

 The policy of Gartner is to provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, citizenship status, age, national origin, ancestry, disability, veteran status, or any other legally protected status and to seek to advance the principles of equal employment opportunity.
   

  

 Gartner is committed to being an Equal Opportunity Employer and offers opportunities to all job seekers, including job seekers with disabilities. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access the Company’s career webpage as a result of your disability. You may request reasonable accommodations by calling Human Resources at +1 (203) 964-0096 or by sending an email to ApplicantAccommodations@gartner.com.
   

  

 Job Requisition ID:100819
   

  

 By submitting your information and application, you confirm that you have read and agree to the country or regional recruitment notice linked below applicable to your place of residence.
   

  

 Gartner Applicant Privacy Link: https://jobs.gartner.com/applicant-privacy-policy

title: Site Reliability Engineer
description:
About Us
 



**At FDJ UNITED, we don't just follow the game, we reinvent it.** 




 FDJ UNITED is one of Europe’s leading betting and gaming operators, with a vast portfolio of iconic brands and a reputation for technological excellence. With more than 5,000 employees and a presence in around fifteen regulated markets, the Group offers a diversified, responsible range of games, both under exclusive rights and open to competition. We set new standards, proving that entertainment and safety can go hand in hand. Here, you’ll work alongside a team of passionate individuals dedicated to delivering the best and safest entertaining experiences for our customers every day.

title: Nordic Equity Derivatives Salesperson
description:
**Your role**
 Are you a people person, with an analytical mindset and deep understanding of financial markets? Do you know how to come up with creative ideas for clients? We are looking for someone who can:
   

  

* provide sales coverage for institutional clients covering the whole spectrum of equity derivative products (delta-1, flow equity derivatives, exotics, risk recycling & quantitative investment solutions)
* introduce, develop and maintain client relationships by understanding their specific needs, which may include goals and objectives, risk tolerance and expectations
* take care of all stages of client management process
* to support any ongoing initiatives that the team is embarking on
* generate new ideas and creative approaches that push the business forward


**Job Type**
 Full Time
   

  

**Job Reference #**
 336557BR
   

  

**City**
 Stockholm
   

  

**Your team**
 You’ll be working in the Nordic Equity Derivatives Sales team in Stockholm. You will be covering Nordic institutional clients with a specific focus Danish institutions and will be providing them access to solutions in equity derivatives and QIS. Your role will require strong client relationships and integration into the existing team and practices, with an ability to support team members, and meet client's requirements across equity derivatives and QIS.
   

  

**Your expertise**
* ideally a minimum of five years of relevant experience
* a strong understanding of financial markets, instruments and strategies
* deep understanding of regulatory environment and its effect on clients
* previous sales experience with Nordic and (particularly) Danish institutional clients
* an excellent communicator, and a collaborative partner with clearly demonstrated ability to work with large and complex groups
* able to thrive in a high intensity and highly demanding role
* eager and hungry to explore and develop new business
* always professional (but you have the human touch)


**About Us**
 UBS is a leading and truly global wealth manager and the leading universal bank in Switzerland. We also provide diversified asset management solutions and focused investment banking capabilities. Headquartered in Zurich, Switzerland, UBS is present in more than 50 markets around the globe.
   

  

 We know that great work is never done alone. That’s why we place collaboration at the heart of everything we do. Because together, we’re more than ourselves. Want to find out more? Visit ubs.com/careers.
   

  

**Join us**
 At UBS, we know that it's our people, with their diverse skills, experiences and backgrounds, who drive our ongoing success. We’re dedicated to our craft and passionate about putting our people first, with new challenges, a supportive team, opportunities to grow and flexible working options when possible. Our inclusive culture brings out the best in our employees, wherever they are on their career journey. And we use artificial intelligence (AI) to work smarter and more efficiently. We also recognize that great work is never done alone. That’s why collaboration is at the heart of everything we do. Because together, we’re more than ourselves.
   

  

 We’re committed to disability inclusion and if you need reasonable accommodation/adjustments throughout our recruitment process, you can always contact us.
   

  

**Disclaimer / Policy statements**
 UBS is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We respect and seek to empower each individual and support the diverse cultures, perspectives, skills and experiences within our workforce.

title: Senior Global Treasury Project Manager
description:
Global Treasury is evolving - and this role plays a key part in that journey. As Senior Global Treasury Project Manager, you help shape modern, efficient Treasury operations while driving initiatives that support Sandvik’s long‑term business strategy.
   

  

**About The Job**
 You lead and deliver high-impact projects across Sandvik. You drive best-practice ways of working—ensuring initiatives are executed in a structured, efficient, and cost-conscious way. The role sits within Group Treasury and acts as a vital link between central functions, divisions, finance organization, and the wider business.
   

  

 Job responsibilities
   

  

* Lead and support different global treasury projects end-to-end.
* Drive transformation initiatives with a focus on standardization, automation, and efficiency
* Project Lead for IT changes, implementations and upgrades
* Coordinate and lead treasury activities related to mergers, acquisitions, and divestitures, including integration workstreams
* Lead implementations of our global credit card programs.
* Ensure high-quality project delivery through strong governance, risk management, and adherence to relevant policies


**Location**
 This position is based at the World Trade Center in Stockholm, Sweden, and follows an office-first way of working.
   

  

**Your profile**
 You bring a strong mix of Treasury expertise and project leadership, combined with the ability to navigate complex, global environments. Your background includes a solid financial foundation and hands-on experience from large, cross-border initiatives.
   

  

 You bring:
   

  

* A degree in Finance, Accounting or a related field
* Extensive experience within Treasury, Corporate Finance, or consulting
* Proven track record of leading complex, global or cross-border projects
* Strong understanding of treasury in general a long with cash management, liquidity, banking structures, and treasury and payment systems
* Fluency in English, both written and spoken


 You´re analytical and structured, with the confidence to drive initiatives forward while keeping stakeholders aligned. Navigating complexity comes naturally, and building strong relationships across functions and cultures is a key part of how you create results. You combine strategic with hands-on execution – delivering real, lasting change.
   

  

**Our culture**
 Our role is clear – through every action, every day, we make the shift and advance the world through engineering. We believe in an inclusive, equal and open-minded culture, and we nurture our diversities to form a solid foundation for achieving great results. Add fair and rewarding benefits, as well as many different career options. Are you intrigued? Visit our website, LinkedIn or Facebook page to get to know us further.
   

  

**Contact information**
 For further information about this position, please contact Anneli Walltott, hiring manager, anneli.waltott@sandvik.com.
   

  

 We’ve already decided on which advertising channels and marketing campaigns we wish to use and respectfully decline any additional contacts in that matter.
   

  

 Union contacts – Sweden
   

  

* Johanna Beijer, Unionen, +46 (0)70 616 18 27
* Erik Knebel, Akademikerföreningen, +46 (0)70 340 47 03
* Peter Olsson-Andrée, Ledarna, +46 (0)70 222 48 55


 Recruitment Specialist: Anton Karlsson
   

  

**How To Apply**
 Send your application no later than June 3rd, 2026. Click apply and include your resume and cover letter in English. Please note that we don’t accept applications by e-mail. Job ID: R0092258.
   

  

 We aim for an open and fair recruitment process and use different tools to ensure an objective assessment. Later in the process, you may be invited to complete a personality and logic test.
   

  

 To learn more about our recruitment process, please visit our career site or contact HR Services at hrservices.sweden@sandvik.com.

title: Account Manager - Intellectual Property
description:
We are looking for an
 **Account Manager** 
 to join our Nordic sales team at Clarivate. This is an exciting opportunity to own and grow a portfolio of high-value accounts across corporates and IP law firms, working with market-leading intellectual property solutions covering the entire IP lifecycle including patent intelligence, trademark data, and IP management systems.
   

  

 This role spans a broad and sophisticated solution portfolio, from outsourced IP services to enterprise-level IP management systems and data-driven offerings. Your work will directly impact how leading organizations innovate, protect, and commercialize their ideas.
   

  

 You will be part of a collaborative, high-performing team focused on driving revenue growth through consultative selling, upsell, and cross-sell strategies. This is for an individual who can act as the CEO of their portfolio and sees opportunity where others see status quo. If you thrive in complex B2B environments and know that the best Account Managers are “hunters in disguise” we would love to speak with you.
   

  

**About You – Experience, Education, Skills, And Accomplishments**
*You are a commercially driven, structured seller with a proven ability to grow accounts and build long-term customer relationships.*
**Must Have Requirements**
* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent relevant work experience
* Minimum 5 years of professional experience, including at least 2 years in B2B sales
* Proven track record of achieving or exceeding sales targets
* Experience in consultative, solution-based selling
* Experience managing customer relationships and driving account growth
* Experienced with value-based selling methodologies (MEDDPICC)
* Fluency in English
* Fluency in at least one Nordic language (Swedish preferred)


**It Would Be Great If You Also Have**
* Experience selling software / SaaS solutions
* Experience in selling outsourced solutions
* Experience working with data, analytics, or intellectual property solutions
* Familiarity with Salesforce or similar CRM systems
* Experience working in complex, multi-stakeholder sales environments


**What will you be doing in this role?**
*This role is measured by your ability to grow, retain, and expand your accounts.*
* Achieve sales goals by meeting targets and proactively identifying opportunities for upselling and cross-selling
* Own and drive revenue growth across your portfolio, with a strong focus on expanding existing customer relationships
* Ensure customers are maximizing the value of their current solutions, identifying gaps, and positioning additional services where relevant
* Support and drive contract renewals, ensuring high retention rates and long-term customer satisfaction
* Manage a defined portfolio of Nordic accounts, developing and executing strategic account plans
* Build and maintain strong customer relationships across multiple stakeholder levels, including senior decision-makers
* Provide accurate sales forecasts through consistent CRM updates and communication with leadership
* Manage opportunities through the full sales cycle — from discovery and solution development to negotiation and closing
* Collaborate with internal stakeholders including solution consultants, subject matter experts, and customer success teams
* Identify new contacts and expand engagement within existing accounts to drive growth
* Maintain strong product knowledge across Clarivate’s portfolio to effectively position solutions
* Actively participate in team meetings, sharing insights and contributing to overall sales strategy
* Travel within the Nordic region to meet customers, strengthen relationships, and drive business growth


**About The Team**
 You will be part of a
 **Nordic sales team focused on high-value, innovation-driven customers** 
 across corporates and IP law firms.
   

  

 The team operates with a strong
 **collaborative mindset** 
 , where strategy, knowledge, and success are shared. You will work closely with internal experts across sales, consulting, and customer success, giving you the support needed to navigate complex deals and deliver value to customers.
   

  

 We combine
 **high expectations on performance, structure, and professionalism** 
 with a supportive and team-oriented culture. The goal is simple: win together, grow together, and continuously improve.
   

  

**Hours of Work**
 This is a
 **full-time, permanent position** 
 based in the Nordics.
   

  

 The role follows standard business hours within your time zone, with flexibility to collaborate across regions when needed.
   

  

 This is a
 **remote position** 
 , with access to work from our Stockholm office
   

  

 At Clarivate, we are committed to providing equal employment opportunities for all qualified persons with respect to hiring, compensation, promotion, training, and other terms, conditions, and privileges of employment. We comply with applicable laws and regulations governing non-discrimination in all locations.

title: Senior Staff Machine Learning Engineer, Content Platform
description:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.
   

  

 The Content Platform team powers the full lifecycle of content across music, podcasts, audiobooks, and emerging formats at Spotify. We ensure that everything from licensed catalog to user-generated content is trusted, safe, and high quality for millions of listeners worldwide. Our systems are responsible for how content is ingested, understood, enriched, governed, and distributed across the platform. As the scale and diversity of content continues to grow—driven by advances in AI and new creation tools—we’re building intelligent systems that can evaluate, manage, and route content reliably at global scale.
   

  

 We’re seeking a Senior Staff Machine Learning Engineer to build and scale foundational ML systems that power content understanding, safety, and decisioning across the platform. In this role, you’ll shape the architecture and technical strategy that ensures content is evaluated, governed, and safely delivered at global scale. This work is foundational to delivering safe, high-quality experiences for both listeners and creators, while enabling new ways to interact with content across Spotify.
   

  

 What You Will Do
   

  

* Shape the machine learning strategy for content understanding and platform-level decisioning
* Build & scale ML systems for classification, moderation, ranking, risk detection across multimodal content
* Develop automated decisioning systems that ensure content quality, integrity, & policy compliance at scale
* Design and deploy models across text, audio, image, and video domains
* Build systems that enable controlled, reliable access to content and metadata for downstream applications
* Collaborate with product, policy, trust & safety teams to operationalize content standards across platform
* Improve automation to reduce manual intervention while maintaining high standards of trust and quality
* Mentor engineers and contribute to best practices in ML engineering, evaluation, and system design


 Who You Are
   

  

* You have strong experience building production-grade machine learning systems at scale
* You are experienced with modern ML frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, or JAX
* You have worked with or are interested in multimodal machine learning
* You understand how to design systems that balance automation with quality, safety, and user experience
* You are comfortable working on complex, ambiguous problems with high impact
* You think in systems, connecting models to platform-level outcomes and user experiences
* You care deeply about data quality, evaluation rigor, and system reliability
* You communicate clearly and influence across technical and non-technical teams


 Where You Will Be
   

  

* This role is based in London or Stockholm
* We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.


 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. Find our AI notice here: https://lifeatspotify.com/ai-notice
   

  

 Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

title: Site Manager till DSV Åkeri
description:
DSV är en dynamisk arbetsplats som främjar samhörighet och mångfald. Vi bedriver vår verksamhet med integritet samt med respekt för olika kulturer och individers värdighet och rättigheter.
   

  

 När du kommer till DSV arbetar du för ett av de allra bäst presterande företagen inom transport- och logistikbranschen.
   

  

 Du kommer att ingå i ett kunnigt bolag med närmare 160 000 anställda i över 90 länder, som arbetar passionerat för att leverera fantastiska kundupplevelser och tjänster av hög kvalitet. Vi strävar efter att ligga i framkant i arbetet mot en mer hållbar framtid för vår bransch och att göra affärer på naturens villkor.
   

  

 DSV Haulage AB (DSV Åkeri) är ett helägt dotterbolag till DSV Road. Vi finns på 21 orter i landet och transporterar årligen 8 miljoner paket och godssändningar. Vår verksamhet består av hämtnings-, distributions- och fjärrtrafik inom DSVs inrikes nätverk för gods och paket samt temperaturkontrollerade varor.
   

  

 DSV Haulage har ca 1100 fordonsenheter och 1100 anställda över hela landet.
   

  

 Ta möjligheten hos DSV att göra något som är viktigt på riktigt! Vi måste röra oss framåt för att vardagens självklarheter kan förbli just självklara. Du kan röra dig med oss mot något ännu mer effektivt, grönare och bättre. Välkommen att skicka in din ansökan.
   

  

 DSV Åkeri i Stockholm söker nu en Site Manager (platschef) som kommer att ansvara för affärsområdet Hämtning och Distribution på vår terminal i Rosersberg.
   

  

 Som Site Manager på DSV Åkeri har du en strategisk och övergripande roll där du har det yttersta ansvaret för att säkerställa leveransen av våra transporttjänster i distrikt Rosersberg med omnejd.
   

  

 Du har ekonomiskt ansvar för ditt affärsområde, vilket innebär att på en övergripande nivå säkerställa att de lokala verksamhetsmålen uppnås genom hög kostnadseffektivitet, produktivitet, kundnöjdhet samt lönsamhet.
   

  

 Utöver det har du också personalansvar för vår transportchef och fordonsansvarige, och du leder arbetet genom underrapporterande ledare som ansvarar för den dagliga driften.
   

  

 Tillsammans utvecklar ni vår anläggning och jobbar löpande för att effektivisera och stärka vår produktion men också för att säkra våra medarbetares arbetsmiljö, trivsel och arbetsglädje. Givet vårt verksamhetsområde ingår även ett omfattande och viktigt arbetsmiljöansvar.
   

  

 Du rapporterar till vår General Manager på DSV Åkeri. Arbetet utgår från vårt kontor i Rosersberg, som ligger i anslutning till vår terminal.
   

  

 Som person tror vi att du har mångårig erfarenhet av ledarskap i roller som innefattat fullständigt ansvar för både ekonomi och personal. Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av transportbranschen men huvudsaken är att du jobbat i en bransch som innefattat komplicerade flöden som t ex logistik eller produktion.
   

  

 Vidare är du van att jobba med KPI:er avseende produktivitet, effektivitet och kvalitet. Du är också en van IT- samt systemanvändare och du behärskar svenska samt engelska i tal och skrift.
   

  

 Dina yrkesmässiga kvalifikationer är viktiga för oss men din personlighet är av minst lika stor betydelse, och vi ser en ledare med intresse för både människor, samarbete, struktur och effektivitet framför oss. Genom din positiva energi och inspirerande approach främjar du både produktivitet och skapar en god arbetsmiljö. Du är bra på att identifiera förbättringsmöjligheter och trivs i en utvecklande och lösningsorienterad roll. Vidare är du proaktiv, bra på att prioritera tid och insats och är bekväm med att fatta beslut.
   

  

 Rollen är en heltidsanställning med tillträde enligt överenskommelse. Placeringsort är Rosersberg i Stockholm, Kromgatan 4. Ersättning enligt kollektivavtal.
   

  

 Vid frågor är du välkommen att kontakta
 *Mathias Hellström* 
 ,
 *General Manager* 
 via
 *mathias.hellstrom@dsv.com* 
 .
   

  

 Ansökningar hanteras löpande, och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdag (18/06 – 2026), så skicka gärna din ansökan så snart som möjligt. Rekryteringsprocessen består bland annat av urval, intervju och referenstagning. Som en del av rekryteringsprocessen genomförs en bakgrundskontroll på aktuella slutkandidater.
   

  

 Välkommen med din ansökan!

title: Global HSE Manager for Service Execution
description:
Every day, we get opportunities to make a positive impact – on our colleagues, partners, customers and society. Together, we’re pioneering the solutions of the future and unlocking the full potential of precious resources. Trusted to act on initiative, we challenge conventional thinking to develop world-leading technologies that inspire progress in vital areas, including energy, food, water and shipping.
   

  

 As we push forward, the innovative, open spirit that fuels our 140-year-old start-up culture and rapid growth also drives our personal growth. So, as we shape a more resourceful, less wasteful world, we build our careers too.
   

  

**About The Job**
 As Global HSE Manager for Service Execution, You will be part of the Global Service Execution Management Team and play a key role in ensuring we protect our people, customers, and business.
   

  

 You will make sure we have clear standards in place, that they are followed in practice, and that we continuously improve how we work with safety.
   

  

 Our service teams often work under time pressure and in demanding conditions. That makes it essential to have a strong and practical approach to safety. Your job is to ensure that risks are understood, managed, and reduced — without slowing down the business unnecessarily.
   

  

 You will work closely with service managers, operations, and technicians across regions. A big part of the role is making sure we meet relevant regulations (such as ISM, ISPS, and MARPOL) and ISO standards (ISO 45001 and 14001), but also helping the organisation move beyond compliance and take real ownership of safety in day-to-day work.
   

  

 This role requires both structure and the ability to influence others. You will set clear ways of working around risk assessments, audits, and incident handling, while also supporting and guiding teams to take responsibility locally.
   

  

**What You’ll Be Responsible For**
* Conducting, reviewing, and managing risk assessments and implementing safety protocols for onboard service
* Maintaining safety files, managing accident database, and preparing compliance reports
* Having and out-side in approach looking af safety onboard vessels and implement safety structure/behavior
* Conducting toolbox talks, training, and promoting a proactive safety culture
* Performing regular audits and inspections of work environment to identify hazards


 You will also contribute to the Global Service Execution Management Team and ensure HSE is part of how decisions are made.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 You are a credible and practical HSE professional who can work with people at all levels of the organisation. You know how to balance clear standards with real-world conditions, and you focus on what actually improves safety — not just what looks good on paper.
   

  

 You are structured in how you work, comfortable in an international setting, and able to handle complexity without losing focus.
   

  

**What You Know**
* A degree in Marine Engineering or a related technical field
* 3–5+ years of experience working with HSE, ideally in a marine or maritime setting
* Good understanding of ISM, MARPOL, ISO 45001, ISO 14001, and similar frameworks
* Experience working across functions and geographies
* Strong communication skills, with the ability to adapt to different audiences


**For more information**
 Please contact Head of Service Operation, Freek van Beuningen, at freekvan.beuningen@alfalaval.com, or Talent Acquisition Manager – Nordic, Frederik Ellegaard, at +45 27 79 82 90.
   

  

 Please send your application no later than 14th of June 2026. If the right candidate is found, we will close the position before. All internal applicants must apply through Workday.

title: Master Thesis, 30hp: Reconstructing saturated ADC samples to detect threats earlier
description:
*Are you a student eager to apply your theoretical knowledge and fresh perspectives to real-world challenges? At Saab, we believe that innovation thrives on new ideas, and your master thesis project could be the spark that ignites our next technological breakthrough.*
*Your role*
 We recognize the immense value that students bring to our company. Your academic rigor, combined with your enthusiasm for cutting-edge technology, allows you to approach problems with a unique and insightful lens. At Saab, you'll have the opportunity to collaborate with experienced engineers and specialists, gaining invaluable practical experience while making a tangible contribution to our growth and development.
   

  

**Background**
*The more antenna and amplifier gain the better – unless it leads to saturation in the analog to digital converter (ADC). To go beyond the normal limits determined by saturation we want to develop methods to estimate and reconstruct the saturated samples.*
 Systems that receive radio frequency signals, such as radars and electronic support measures (ESM), want to detect as distant and weak signals as possible to give warnings as early as possible, and they therefore want as much gain as possible. This can lead to problems if a much stronger signal appears, or if several signals happen to constructively interfere. In those cases, the received signal can be stronger than what the ADC can sample, leading to clipping/saturation where the amplitude of those digital samples is reduced to the ADC limit.
   

  

 Saturation can be seen as the addition of a signal that is the inverse of the clipped amplitude from the samples. This leads in the best case to added white noise which reduces performance in a predictable way, but saturation can also lead to correlated noise which is much more difficult to handle. To avoid these problems gain is normally reduced until no or almost no saturation happens.
   

  

**Description Of The Master Thesis**
 To avoid having to reduce gain to avoid saturation we want to be able to handle a greater degree of saturation. By assuming there is some structure to the received signal (such as signals being limited to only certain frequencies and noise on other frequencies), it’s possible to estimate the value of saturated samples so that the structure remains as unaffected as possible. You can then replace the values of the saturated samples with the estimated values before doing any other processing.
   

  

 Your task will be to investigate different methods to reconstruct estimated ADC samples, assess their strengths and weaknesses, and how they can be effectively implemented on processing hardware such as FPGA, GPU, or DSP.
   

  

 The thesis can also (depending on interest and time) include:
   

  

* Considering different signal environments with differently structured signals
* Automatically adapting or changing methods to work well for the current signal environment
* Determining an optimal gain for the amplifier for the current signal environment
* Optimizing the FPGA/GPU/DSP implementation
* Finding methods for systems with multiple antennas in an antenna array


*Your profile*
 You are studying engineering physics, math, electrical engineering, or similar, and are approaching the end of your education and will start your thesis job during autumn 2026.
   

  

 The project is suitable for a student who has taken courses in signal processing. Courses in microwave propagation are beneficial, but not necessary.
   

  

 We provide the support and guidance you need to translate your theoretical knowledge into practical solutions. Join us and become a driving force behind Saab's technological advancements!
   

  

*This position requires that you pass a security vetting based on the current regulations around/of security protection. For positions requiring security clearance additional obligations on citizenship may apply.*
 Kindly observe that this is an ongoing recruitment process and that the position might be filled before the closing date of the advertisement.
   

  

*What you will be a part of*
 Explore a wealth of possibilities. Take on challenges, create smart inventions, and grow beyond. This is a place for curious minds, brave pioneers, and everyone in between. Together, we achieve the extraordinary, each bringing our unique perspectives. Your part matters.
   

  

 Saab is a leading defence and security company with an enduring mission, to help nations keep their people and society safe. Empowered by its 26,100 talented people, Saab constantly pushes the boundaries of technology to create a safer and more sustainable world.
   

  

 Saab designs, manufactures and maintains advanced systems in aeronautics, weapons, command and control, sensors and underwater systems. Saab is headquartered in Sweden. It has major operations all over the world and is part of the domestic defence capability of several nations. Read more about us here

 15-06-2026
